# Spec elicitation & planning depth — pushing past /grill-me

- **Date:** 2026-07-06
- **Method:** 11 cloned popular repos mined by parallel analysts; file paths cite the clones at `/Users/dwijen/repos/<name>`. Local project references cite `/Users/dwijen/repos/cc-agent-harness` (the repo as named at mining time; renamed `outrigger` 2026-07-13 — cited v1 paths exist at git tag `v1-attic`).
- **Question under test (operator's hypothesis, quoted):** *"thorough, detailed, granular technical design specs have a big impact on overall wall-clock time, and will significantly improve efficiency by avoiding failures/retries due to spec ambiguity. Could we push the envelope on the /grill-me skill (and similar skills focused on clarifying details and decisions in planning)?"*

## Headline findings

1. Five of eleven repos ship a real interrogation loop; the rest are one-shot plan-confirm, plan *files* without elicitation, or nothing. The pattern converged independently at least three times (Pocock, gstack, superpowers) — strong revealed-preference evidence that sophisticated operators want relentless questioning.
2. **Nobody in the sample has a machine-checkable determinacy exit bar.** Every interview ends on a conversational judgment ("user confirms shared understanding", "agent believes it understands", "enough details collected"). outrigger's (v1, then cc-agent-harness) `python3 -m harness.planning ready` gate is unique in this sample.
3. **Nobody has spec-to-test traceability.** The closest artifacts are review-time spec-compliance checks; no repo maps interview answers → tests, because no repo has held-out tests at all.
4. **The wall-clock claim is measured nowhere in these 11 repos.** One author-run, confounded speed claim (superpowers), one measured *counter*-datapoint (planning-with-files: the planning skill costs +68% tokens), and a lot of "try it and see." The best evidence for the hypothesis is actually in-house: pilot-3's 8h25m mid-firing stall from a foreseeable ambiguity park.

---

## 1. Inventory: what question-asking machinery exists

### Tier A — adaptive interrogation loops (the /grill-me family)

**mattpocock/skills — the original `/grill-me`.** The engine is a 13-line model-invoked primitive: "interrogate the user relentlessly about every branch of the design tree, one question at a time, each with a recommended answer; explore the codebase instead of asking when possible," with an explicit stop-gate — do not enact until the user confirms shared understanding (`/Users/dwijen/repos/skills/skills/productivity/grilling/SKILL.md`; wrappers at `skills/productivity/grill-me/SKILL.md`, `skills/engineering/grill-with-docs/SKILL.md`). `/grill-with-docs` composes `/domain-modeling` so a CONTEXT.md glossary and ADRs are written inline as decisions land. Downstream: `/to-prd` (synthesizes the conversation, no re-interview), `/to-issues` (tracer-bullet slices with blocked-by edges), `/wayfinder` for beyond-one-session planning with a "fog of war" section for questions you cannot yet phrase (`skills/in-progress/wayfinder/SKILL.md`). One buried gem: `loop-me` states the sample's only crisp determinacy criterion — *"A workflow spec is done when an implementer agent could build it without asking a single question"* (`skills/in-progress/loop-me/SKILL.md`) — but nothing checks it.

**gstack — the most engineered interrogator.** `/office-hours` runs six forcing questions **one at a time with a hard STOP after each**, explicit anti-sycophancy rules ("never say: that's an interesting approach"), push-twice patterns, stage-based routing (pre-product asks Q1–Q3 only), an escape hatch that concedes after two pushbacks, a premise-challenge phase requiring explicit agree/disagree, and an optional Codex cold-read second opinion (`/Users/dwijen/repos/gstack/office-hours/SKILL.md` lines 1014–1396). `/spec` is a five-phase interrogation (why → scope → technical → draft → file) where the agent **must Grep/Read evidence before asking Phase-3 questions**, demands quantification ("several files is not acceptable"), and gates the output behind a Codex quality score — specs under 7/10 are blocked (`spec/SKILL.md` lines 800–990). Two unique satellites: a decision-brief question format with dual-scale effort labels ("human: ~2 days / CC: ~15 min") and per-option Completeness X/10 scores (`office-hours/SKILL.md` AskUserQuestion Format), and `/plan-tune`'s question-preference learning — stable question_ids, a per-question never-ask/always-ask store enforced by a PreToolUse hook, distilled from past answers with user approval (`plan-tune/SKILL.md`). Assessment from the evidence: persona-driven *product* interrogation, "not determinacy-driven spec closure — it does not produce a ratified machine-checkable plan ledger that gates the build."

**obra/superpowers — interrogation chained into plan-for-a-stranger.** `brainstorming` has a HARD-GATE (no code, no scaffolding until a design is approved — explicitly including projects "too simple to need a design"), clarifying questions strictly one per message (multiple-choice preferred), 2–3 approaches with a recommendation, sectioned design with per-section approval, a placeholder/contradiction/ambiguity self-review, and an explicit user review gate (`/Users/dwijen/repos/superpowers/skills/brainstorming/SKILL.md`). `writing-plans` then targets an engineer with "zero context and questionable taste": exact file paths, complete code in every step, Consumes/Produces interface blocks per task, a Global Constraints header copied verbatim from the spec, 2–5 minute checkbox steps, and a hard "No Placeholders" rule — no TBD, no "add appropriate error handling," no "similar to Task N" (`skills/writing-plans/SKILL.md`). Optional fresh-context document-reviewer subagents exist for both stages (`spec-document-reviewer-prompt.md`, `plan-document-reviewer-prompt.md`). Still: the plan is a markdown doc; "no determinacy criterion or ratified machine-readable plan ledger."

**alirezarezvani/claude-skills — the derivative cluster.** Vendors Pocock's grill-me with attribution and adds scripts (`decision_tree_extractor.py`, `question_generator.py`, `grill_session_tracker.py`) and a `references/when_to_stop_grilling.md` (`/Users/dwijen/repos/claude-skills/engineering/grill-me/skills/grill-me/SKILL.md`). `grill-with-docs` adds an ADR triple criterion (record only if hard-to-reverse + surprising + real trade-off) and validators that cross-check glossary terms against actual code usage, flagging dead terms as *opening grill questions* (`engineering/grill-with-docs/`). `spec-driven-workflow` enforces "NO CODE WITHOUT AN APPROVED SPEC" with RFC 2119 language and Given/When/Then criteria. `zero-hallucination-coder` contributes two determinacy tools: a KNOWN/INFERRED/UNKNOWN codebase map with the hard rule "never write code that depends on an UNKNOWN," but its DISCUSS phase asks 5 questions **in a single batch** — the anti-pattern the one-at-a-time family exists to avoid (`engineering/zero-hallucination-coder/`). None gate a build: "the interview output is prose, not a gate."

**santifer/career-ops — the pattern transposed out of software.** `modes/interview.md`: "Rule: Ask exactly ONE question at a time. Never present a wall of questions," digging for measurable outcomes; `modes/interview/practice.md` is a full grill-style practice interviewer with structured per-answer feedback. Its stop condition — "enough new details have been collected" — is the fuzziest in the sample, and the repo's own README admits the consequence: "the first evaluations won't be great." A fuzzy exit bar producing weak downstream output is a small natural experiment in favor of determinacy criteria.

### Tier B — one-shot plan-then-confirm

**everythingclaudecode (50K+ stars claimed).** `commands/plan.md` + `agents/planner.md`: restate requirements, phased plan, hard-stop "WAITING FOR CONFIRMATION" — with only a one-line "ask clarifying questions if needed." The analyst's grep for grill/interview/"one question"/AskUserQuestion across all commands and skills "finds nothing of that shape." `skills/blueprint/SKILL.md` is the interesting artifact: cold-executable plans where every step carries a self-contained context brief, verification commands, and exit criteria, plus a dependency graph and an adversarial strongest-model review gate — plan *quality* machinery without elicitation machinery.

### Tier C — plan files or nothing (absence is the finding)

- **planning-with-files:** plan artifacts, hooks, attestation, completion gates — and **zero elicitation**. `/plan` just instantiates three template files; grep for interview/grill/brainstorm/clarify confirms nothing exists. A "brainstorm-before-plan gate" was proposed (item #8, `proposal_v2_40.md`) and explicitly **deferred** (`CHANGELOG.md` line 346: it "deserves its own focused cycle"); the most celebrated community fork (devis) exists precisely to add an interview-first workflow. Documented, unmet demand.
- **no-mistakes:** no pre-build planning at all; instead *after-the-fact intent capture* — the driving agent must pass `--intent`, with fallback inference from local session transcripts by file-overlap scoring (`/Users/dwijen/repos/no-mistakes/internal/intent/matcher.go`). Spec recovery for validation, not spec elicitation.
- **andrej-karpathy-skills:** Principle 1 "Think Before Coding" — state assumptions, ask when confused — is pure exhortation; "NO one-question-at-a-time elicitation loop, no spec artifact, no ratification step." The analyst's framing: this repo is "the demand signal for it, not the supply."
- **caveman:** none in-repo (its author's grill-me lives in the sibling JuliusBrussee/skills repo, per README line 233).
- **ruflo (~63k stars):** SPARC's Specification phase is agent-driven (researcher/analyzer modes), a GOAP A* planner decomposes goals — "NO /grill-me-style skill exists"; the operator is never interrogated.

---

## 2. How deep do they actually go?

| Repo / skill | Cadence | Adaptivity | Stop condition | Artifact | Gates the build? |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| pocock `grilling` | 1 q/turn, recommended answer | walks design tree, explores repo first | user confirms shared understanding | conversation → optional PRD/issues | no |
| gstack `/office-hours` | 1 q/turn, hard STOP each | stage routing, push-twice, concession rule | six questions exhausted + premise challenge | design doc | no |
| gstack `/spec` | phased | evidence-required before Phase-3 questions | Codex score ≥7/10 (LLM gate) | filed issue/spec | no (gates the *spec*, not a build) |
| superpowers `brainstorming`→`writing-plans` | 1 q/turn, multiple-choice preferred | approach comparison, per-section approval | self-review + user sign-off | dated spec + zero-context plan doc | HARD-GATE on coding, but prose-enforced |
| claude-skills `grill-me` | 1 q/turn | depth-first tree + tracker scripts | `when_to_stop_grilling.md` heuristics | prose | no |
| career-ops `interview` | 1 q/turn | digs for measurables | "enough new details" | profile files | n/a |
| ecc `plan.md` | single shot | "if needed" | user confirmation | plan text | no |
| planning-with-files | — (no elicitation) | — | — | attested plan files | yes (completion gate, not determinacy) |
| outrigger-v1 `plan-build` | 1 q/turn, recommended answer | dependency-ordered tree, explore-don't-ask, delegated-decision conversion | **determinacy bar: spec-only test-author could write held-out tests with no guessing; two clean sweeps** | PLAN.md + tasks.json + per-task specs + floors + conventions | **yes: preflight + content-bound ratify stamp + `planning ready` exit-0** |

Three depth observations:

1. **The question mechanics are largely solved and convergent.** One-question-at-a-time, recommended-answer-attached, explore-don't-ask appear independently in Pocock, gstack, superpowers, claude-skills, career-ops, and plan-build. There is little envelope left to push on *asking style* — the frontier moved.
2. **Stop conditions are the weak joint everywhere.** The sample's exit bars are, in increasing rigor: fuzzy prose (career-ops) → user-felt agreement (pocock, superpowers) → heuristic reference doc (claude-skills) → LLM quality score (gstack `/spec`'s 7/10 Codex gate — the only *enforced* exit in the sample, but it scores writing quality, not determinacy) → plan-build's test-author-could-write-tests-without-guessing bar. Nobody executes their bar mechanically.
3. **Plan enforcement and plan elicitation never co-occur** — except in the harness. planning-with-files has attestation (SHA-256-locked plans, injection refused on tamper — `scripts/attest-plan.sh`, `inject-plan.sh`) and a Stop-hook completion gate, but no interview. The grilling family has interviews but prose outputs no tool refuses to build without. gstack has both halves in separate skills that don't compose into a gate.

---

## 3. Evidence on the wall-clock claim

**Direct measurement of "granular specs → wall-clock": zero repos.** What exists:

- **For (author-run, confounded):** superpowers v6.0.0 claims "roughly twice as fast, almost 50% fewer tokens" for the whole methodology — author-run evals with the in-repo caveat "these numbers won't hold on every harness" (`/Users/dwijen/repos/superpowers/RELEASE-NOTES.md`). The methodology bundles brainstorming + zero-context plans + SDD + TDD, so nothing isolates spec granularity. Marketing-adjacent but the most serious attempt in the sample (N=5 run gates, no-guidance controls in `writing-skills/SKILL.md`).
- **For (indirect, measured):** superpowers' strict-cost spec decomposes a ~$13 SDD run and names **review-loop count as the biggest run-to-run cost variance** (`docs/superpowers/specs/2026-06-10-strict-cost-sdd-design.md`). Review loops fire on spec-compliance findings (Missing/Extra/Misunderstood — `task-reviewer-prompt.md`), so spec ambiguity plausibly drives the dominant variance term. Plausible, not proven.
- **For (in-house, the strongest datapoint anywhere):** the harness's own record — both pilot-3 halts were foreseeable ambiguity parks, one costing an **8h25m mid-firing stall**, and pilot-3-v2 "discovered its convention mid-firing via 14 fixture errors" (`/Users/dwijen/repos/cc-agent-harness/.claude/skills/plan-build/SKILL.md`, Preflight and conventions.json sections). These are actual wall-clock losses attributable to specific spec indeterminacies — and both drove mechanical fixes (I19 preflight, P3v2-4 conventions pinning). This is single-source per the standing evidence rule, but it is *ours* and it is causal in a way nothing in the 11 repos is.
- **Against (measured):** planning-with-files' own eval found the planning skill **costs +68% tokens** (`docs/evals.md`, Test 1), and its headline 96.7% benchmark measures file-pattern fidelity, not outcome quality. Planning artifacts have measured overhead and unmeasured benefit — in the only repo honest enough to measure.
- **Against (attribution):** the "single most expensive failure observed" in superpowers SDD was not spec ambiguity — it was controllers losing their place after compaction and re-dispatching completed task sequences (`subagent-driven-development/SKILL.md`, Durable Progress). Resume/ledger failures compete with spec ambiguity as the top wall-clock killer.
- **Marketing to ignore:** claude-skills' "60-80% of defects originate from requirements" (unmeasured rule-of-thumb); everything Karpathy-skills asserts; gstack's compression tables.
- **Measurement is confounded by variance:** the researcher's own ~30x same-task token-variance finding, corroborated by superpowers ("N=5 runs, not 1 — single-run gates were this campaign's weakest methodology") and ruflo's seeded-bootstrap CI gate on improvement deltas (`v3/@claude-flow/cli/src/services/harness-improvement-ledger.ts`), means single-run before/after comparisons of spec depth are noise.

---

## 4. Envelope-pushing ideas, checked against what exists

| Concept | Exists in sample? | Closest prior art |
|---|---|---|
| Ambiguity taxonomy | **nowhere** | superpowers' placeholder/contradiction/ambiguity self-review triple; its `writing-skills` "Match the Form to the Failure" table (classify the failure type, then choose the guidance form) is the meta-pattern to copy |
| Decision ledgers | partial | gstack `decisions.jsonl` — append-only decide/supersede/redact events, computed active set, "do not silently re-litigate" (`lib/gstack-decision.ts`); ADR triple criterion (claude-skills grill-with-docs); career-ops doctrine-by-issue-link |
| Spec-determinacy exit bar | **nowhere machine-checked** | loop-me's one-line criterion (unchecked); gstack's Codex 7/10 gate (enforced but scores prose quality); plan-build's bar (checked by self-sweep, not by tool) |
| Contract-first artifacts | partial, prose-only | superpowers Consumes/Produces blocks + No Placeholders; ecc blueprint per-step exit criteria; nothing typed or tool-validated |
| Spec-to-test traceability | **nowhere** | pocock code-review Spec axis "quoting spec lines" (review-time, not plan-time); superpowers Missing/Extra/Misunderstood verdicts; spec-driven-workflow's "acceptance criteria are the test plan" (aspiration, no linkage) |

Concrete envelope pushes, ordered by novelty-times-evidence:

1. **Executable determinacy probe (nowhere in sample).** Operationalize loop-me's criterion mechanically: before ratification, dispatch a fresh-context, spec-only agent (the natural choice in the harness: a dry-run validator/test-author) and count its questions. Zero questions across the task specs = determinate; each question is a discovered ambiguity fed back into the interview. This turns plan-build's step 5 bar ("a spec-only test-author could write held-out tests with no guessing") from a self-assessed sweep into a measured gate, and it produces the ambiguity corpus item 2 needs. The harness's preflight (`python3 -m harness.planning preflight`) already proves the pattern for statically-evaluable gates; this extends it to semantic ones.
2. **Ambiguity taxonomy with per-class question forms.** Nobody has one. Seed it empirically from the harness's own failures: convention/environment ambiguity (pilot-3-v2's 14 fixture errors), profile/floor collisions, H9 handoff ambiguities, plus the classes plan-build already walks (boundary, permission, error path, invariant, tenancy). Each class gets a canned question form and a coverage checklist the interview must clear — the interview stops being a tree-walk of vibes and becomes coverage against a typed checklist. gstack's stage-routing shows conditional question sets work; superpowers' Form-to-Failure table shows classification-first improves prompt effectiveness measurably.
3. **Spec-to-test traceability IDs (nowhere in sample, and only the harness *can* build it).** Give every acceptance criterion in `plan/specs/<task-id>.md` a stable ID; the blind validator tags each vault test with the criterion IDs it covers; the merge gate reports uncovered criteria and orphan tests. No repo in the sample can do this because none has held-out tests — this is a genuinely unique combination of existing harness assets, not new machinery.
4. **Event-sourced decision ledger with anti-re-litigation.** Adopt gstack's decide/supersede/redact shape for PLAN.md's decisions log (plan-build's `DECISION (delegated)` rule already captures delegation; supersede semantics would make re-ratification diffs first-class), and surface "recent decisions — do not silently re-litigate" in worker kickoff blocks.
5. **Question-preference learning to pay for the extra depth.** The realistic cost of pushing the envelope is operator fatigue — the interview serializes on a human. gstack built `/plan-tune` specifically for this: stable question_ids, never-ask/always-ask preferences, hook-enforced auto-decide with a user-origin gate. Import the idea before deepening the interview, or depth will be resented. Related: a triviality bypass (karpathy's "Tradeoff" escape hatch) vs superpowers' explicit refusal to allow one ("even projects too simple to need a design") is a live design disagreement — the harness's regime tags (`chore`/`thinking`/`long_horizon`) suggest a middle path: interview depth conditioned on task regime.
6. **Attestation-gated spec injection.** planning-with-files SHA-256-locks the plan at attest time and refuses to inject tampered or unattested bodies (`attest-plan.sh`, `inject-plan.sh`). plan-build's ratify stamp is already content-bound; extending the check to the worker-consumption side (workers refuse specs whose hash ≠ ratified snapshot) closes the loop for ~zero cost.

Also worth stealing, smaller: recommended-answer-first with dual-scale effort labels and Completeness X/10 per option (gstack AUQ format); anti-sycophancy phrasing rules and the concede-after-two-pushbacks calibration (office-hours); evidence-before-question (gstack `/spec` Phase 3 mandatory code reading — plan-build's "explore, don't ask" is the same rule, gstack adds *proof of exploration*); wayfinder's fog-of-war slot for questions you cannot yet phrase (maps to plan-build's provisional phases); KNOWN/INFERRED/UNKNOWN mapping as interview instrumentation (zero-hallucination-coder).

---

## 5. What the operator's project adds

Mapping outrigger (then cc-agent-harness) assets onto the sample's verified gaps:

- **The only machine-checkable planning gate.** `planning preflight` → content-bound `ratify` → `ready` exit-0 refusing the build-loop (`.claude/skills/plan-build/SKILL.md`, `harness/planning`). Confirmed absent in all 11 repos — three analysts independently noted "nothing like `python3 -m harness.planning ready`."
- **A determinacy bar with a consumer.** Every other interview's output is read by the same agent (or a persona reviewer). plan-build's bar is defined by an *adversarial downstream consumer* — the spec-only held-out test-author. That makes determinacy falsifiable: validator confusion = spec defect. No sampled repo has this feedback path because none separates the test-author's context.
- **Spec-to-test traceability is uniquely buildable here** (item 3 above) — it requires vault-held tests authored from the spec, which only this project has.
- **The measurement capability the hypothesis needs.** The 30x-variance finding plus ledger-instrumented firings position the harness to actually *test* spec-depth-vs-wall-clock (N-run, per-task paired deltas, bootstrap CI per ruflo's `harness-improvement-ledger.ts` pattern) — turning §3's evidence vacuum into a publishable result either way.
- **What to import in return:** question-preference learning (gstack), attestation-gated injection (planning-with-files), event-sourced decisions (gstack), fog-of-war provisional slots (wayfinder), proof-of-exploration before questions (gstack `/spec`).

Contribution surface this doc exposes (each verified absent upstream): a determinacy-checklist reference for claude-skills' grill-me (`references/when_to_stop_grilling.md` is the hook, and the analyst rated it an easy trust-building PR); the deferred brainstorm-before-plan gate in planning-with-files (maintainer already wants it — `proposal_v2_40.md` item #8); a `/grill` command for everythingclaudecode (its planning tops out at plan-then-confirm); a standalone grill-me-with-exit-bar plugin for the karpathy-skills/marketplace audience.

---

## 6. Verdict on the operator's hypothesis

**On the causal claim: directionally supported, empirically unproven — including by us, mostly.** No repo in this sample measures spec granularity against wall-clock time; the one project that measured its planning machinery at all found it *costs* 68% more tokens with unquantified benefit (planning-with-files `docs/evals.md`). The honest strongest evidence is in-house and anecdotal-but-causal: pilot-3's 8h25m stall and pilot-3-v2's 14 fixture errors were each a single unresolved spec question turning into hours of firing time — exactly the failure-and-retry mechanism the hypothesis names. Superpowers' finding that review-loop count dominates run cost variance points the same way. So: believe the hypothesis provisionally, but treat "big impact" as an open measurement, not a fact — and note the competing wall-clock killer the evidence surfaced (resume/ledger failures, superpowers' "single most expensive failure observed"), which spec depth does not fix.

**On "push the envelope on /grill-me": yes, but not by grilling harder.** The asking mechanics (one-question-at-a-time, recommended answers, explore-don't-ask) are convergent, commoditized prior art across five repos — plan-build already matches or exceeds all of them. More questions per interview has a real, evidenced cost: the interview serializes on the operator (wall-clock includes *operator* time), and the most sophisticated player in the space (gstack) found interrogation fatigue serious enough to build an entire preference-learning subsystem around it. Pushing the envelope as "longer, more relentless interviews" would be a bad trade.

The envelope that is actually open — verified empty across all 11 repos — is on the *exit* side, not the *asking* side: **(a)** a machine-executable determinacy probe (dry-run spec-only test-author; count its questions), **(b)** a typed ambiguity taxonomy driving coverage-checklist questioning, **(c)** criterion-ID spec-to-test traceability enforced at the merge gate, and **(d)** an actual N-run experiment attributing retries/parks/stalls to ambiguity classes, so the hypothesis graduates from folklore to measurement. All four compose from assets the harness already has and no sampled competitor can replicate without first building blind validation. That is where to push.
